title: 019f8586-dc22-7096-98e1-1d3792b94ed0

overview.description: Small baked Argentine-style empanadas filled with savoury beef, onion, paprika, cumin and olives.

method: Mix the flour and salt in a large bowl.
Rub in the cold butter until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
Add the egg and enough cold water to bring the mixture together into a firm dough.
Knead briefly until smooth.
Wrap and chill for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat.
Cook the onion and red pepper until softened.
Add the garlic, paprika, cumin, oregano and chilli flakes if using.
Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the beef mince and cook until browned, breaking it up thoroughly.
Add the beef stock and simmer until almost completely reduced.
Stir through the chopped olives and hard-boiled egg.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Allow the filling to cool completely.
Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan).
Divide the pastry into 12 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a circle approximately 10–12cm in diameter.
Place a spoonful of filling in the centre of each circle.
Fold the pastry over to form a half-moon.
Press the edges firmly together and crimp with a fork.
Place on a baking tray and brush with beaten egg.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until golden brown.
Allow to cool slightly before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Empanadas are found throughout Latin America, Spain and the Philippines, but Argentine empanadas have developed many distinct regional styles and fillings.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a simple filled pastry, create a flavourful minced beef filling and shape small pastries for batch cooking.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A freezer-friendly snack that gives the children something different from sausage rolls while teaching the useful skill of making and shaping filled pastry.
chapman-tips: The filling should be completely cool before it goes into the pastry.
Cook the filling until almost dry so the pastry stays crisp.
Don't overfill the empanadas or they will be difficult to seal.
Keep the pastry chilled if the kitchen is warm.
Make a few different crimping patterns if you are making multiple fillings so they can be identified easily.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze assembled, unbaked empanadas on a tray until solid, then transfer to a freezer bag or container. Bake from frozen at 200°C (180°C fan), adding approximately 10 minutes to the cooking time.
notes.leftovers: Cooked empanadas are good cold or can be reheated in the oven to restore the crisp pastry.
